name: WOOVO Phuket Patong – by Swandor
detail: Good located in a side street at patong, only a 10 minutes walk to the beach. Nice view to the ocean from the 8th floor. The rooms were big and clean, modern and new. Would really recommend this hotel.

name: Crest Resort & Pool Villas
detail: When we drove up to this resort, I was thinking wow this is an amazing property, wish it's where we are staying. Then I saw the sign and we all got excited. It has some serious curb appeal. We drove up, parked our car and saw a lot of construction still going on. Security called for 2 golf carts to come down and pick up our luggage and the 3 of us. We took a steep and dangerous ride, passengers in the back need to hang on for dear life. The reception desk is located outdoors under some of the architecture. A pool wraps around the front of some of the pool access rooms. It's beautiful, but in an Ikea sort of way. Our room, the sea view room, while somewhat stylish looks great with a wonderful view but that's just the surface. If you stay in the room, you will find that there's only about 2 outlets inside and 1 outlet on the balcony. The wifi signal was a bit weak but I speedtested it and it measured in at 5Mb up and down. The finishes in the room are pretty low quality but I've found that with a lot of Phuket hotels. The restroom facilities are bad as they give you pretty much no privacy. I wouldn't recommend it for a family stay. With only 2 outlets, you have to plug in the hair dryer outside of the restroom area. The doors are glass and frosted in some parts but lend very little privacy. The toilet room is separated from the shower but the door only goes part way up letting every little noise you make out into the living space. Water pressure was low on the rainfall showerhead. The bathroom counter hardly had any usable space, you better learn to live tight. Beds are super firm. There wasn't much consideration with regard to safety so you better watch your kids. The ride up to the property was dangerous as is the property itself. Every doorway has a ledge so the moment you step out, you are falling down and you can really hurt yourself if you're not careful. The pool is amazing, views of 2 beaches and it has a bar area. Some rooms will have pool access at thei

name: Niranapa Boutique Resort
detail: The restaurant served very good food with a lot of options.

name: GP House Phuket
detail: Great location, great service, cheap price, amazingly clean.

name: Lub d Phuket Patong
detail: This is one of the best hostels, more like a hotel to me, that I stayed at in Thailand. I reserved the smallest private room and I was extremely happy with it: it was super clean, modern and functional. Everything worked 100%. The bathroom was clean and immaculate. Water temperature and pressure was excellent. The staff was friendly and excellent as well. I highly suggest that you utilize their inhouse travel services (STA Travel). Toni from STA, booked a full day island tour that was affordable and luxurious. That's all I'm going to say. If you can opt for a better package, go with it, you won't regret it. Also worth mentioning, is that there are so many activities within the hostel that can allow you to relax, socialize and network with people. Somedays, you can just stay inside the 'hostel', away from the hustle of Phuket within a nice environment and still enjoy yourself. Location is excellent: next to a lot of restaurants, food stalls, massage parlor and Walking ST.

name: Amata Patong
detail: We enjoyed every minute of our stay in Amata. We had a modern design Grand deluxe room and the view was amazing. Love the swimming pool and the staff are also friendly. The hotel is very close to Jungceylon Shopping Centre and there are many food court/Banzaan food market nearby if you want to save money. Also close to Bangla Road but still far enough from the noise of the club. Should try the Planet Massage just right next to the hotel on the main building. You will not miss it, as it is located right next to the swimming pool. They offer a proper massage and you can really fell the pressure because the masseur have good experience. The boy at the tour agent on the RIGHT HAND SIDE of the Planet Massage also offers the best and cheaper deal for any tour around Phuket (we have tried to look around the area and so far he always offered the best deal). The only bad experience we had in Phuket was during the airport transfer to the hotel. We paid for 130Baht per person for the mini van at the Phuket Airport to be transferred to our hotel and the driver was driving like crazy, every passenger in the van was scared. It seems that he was stress or overwork and just want to go home. but this driver was really really BAD, dangerous & rude. Very shocking..... as they still allow him to drive. The driver also stopped the van for half an hour in their company's office to offer tour package for all the passengers (C'MON....ALL OF US WERE ALREADY TIRED & it was late at 9pm from a long 10 hour flight from Oz & we just want to go straight to our hotel). I would suggest if you don't mind to pay a bit more, you can just pay for 800baht for a taxi from the agency at the airport to the hotel, OR make a booking from Amata Resort for a taxi to pick you up from the airport as the hotel also charge the same 800Baht for a taxi transfer.

name: Wyndham Sea Pearl Resort Phuket
detail: The hotel felt a little posh as we drove up the driveway and saw the entrance-lobby. Staff seemed professional. We were a little taken aback by the compulsory security deposit to check-in. I suppose some hotels require it, some don't. The room was very spacious. It had sliding panels to separate the bedroom from the living room and kitchenette. Though not quite mosaic, I was quite fond of the colour-tiled wall on the outside of the bathroom. However, the colours in the living room were quite bold and personally felt they clashed a little. It wasn't a huge deal. We spent more time in the jacuzzi and in bed watching TV. There were a number of pools at this hotel and all seemed to have drink promotions throughout the day. Fun! The hotel is a little up a hill, and there wasn't any food or anything going on that seemed walking distance just outside the hotel, but it wasn't difficult to get a tuk-tuk to the heart of the action - just a little tourist-priced. Think the standard was about 300 baht. Our stay was comfortable, so we would tell people about it. But then there are so many great hotels in Phuket that we may endeavour to stay at different ones for every trip we personally take there.

name: Saba Suites Patong Phuket
detail: Location is nearest to walkiing street just 2mins aways.

name: Hallo Patong Hotel
detail: The best so far i got hotel at phuket

name: The Kee Resort & Spa
detail: Its a lovely & awesome hotel in Phuket. Located in a very very good area. As what I say about the cons are , just one walk down to the lobby & the bars are just in front of you. If you love night life & drinking. Just a short walk you can reach shopping area and the beach. Nice lovely sky lounge on the top of the hotel. With nice voice singers. Nice breakfast in the hotel too. Did not have dinner in hotel cause the seafood restaurants are just so near beside hotel. Definitely worth the money staying in this hotel.
